purpose: Fine-grained audit of AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, and pyproject.toml against the known crack graph so root instructions route future fixes through reconciled repo truth instead of stale or over-broad repo logic.
audit_method: Read current AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, and pyproject.toml.
Compared root instructions against crack-graph root causes and fix waves.

Searched for blocking language around production readiness, stale index status, summary-only behavior, helper manifests, VBT642 catalog-only mode, MCP/runtime precedence, source readiness, and evidence semantics.
Patched root control files to encode repo-truth reconciliation, not blind obedience to stale repo surfaces.

findings_and_repairs.title: Root files treated repo guidance as decisive authority without an explicit crack-graph reconciliation layer.
findings_and_repairs.risk_before_patch: Agents could follow stale helper manifests, query profiles, VBT642 catalog-only policies, scanner findings, or index status as blockers even when the current audit proved those surfaces need repair.
repair: Added AGENTS.md Repo Truth Reconciliation section.
Added SKILL.md reconciliation rules.
Added pyproject.toml [tool.vbtprobuild.repo_truth_reconciliation].
findings_and_repairs.optimized_fix_plan_effect: Every future fix wave now starts from a reconciled evidence graph: hard safety invariants remain hard, but stale status/audit/catalog-only surfaces are evidence until focused tests or validators fail.

findings_and_repairs.title: Catalog-only VBT tool and VBT642 surfaces could be read as universal no-runtime blockers.
findings_and_repairs.risk_before_patch: The root docs told agents to query VBT642/tool policies but did not warn that those surfaces currently encode catalog_reference mode as if it were the only mode.
repair: AGENTS.md now states catalog-only/no-runtime VBT tool and VBT642 policies describe catalog_reference mode unless an activated private-runtime research overlay explicitly says otherwise.
SKILL.md now repeats the same catalog_reference versus activated_private_runtime distinction.
pyproject.toml mode_semantics records the same rule for machine-readable tooling.
findings_and_repairs.optimized_fix_plan_effect: Wave 3 overlay, Wave 6 VBT642, and Wave 7 VBT tool work can proceed without weakening catalog-mode tests or being stopped by old no-runtime semantics.

findings_and_repairs.title: Root docs did not explicitly warn that source readiness is currently ambiguous.
findings_and_repairs.risk_before_patch: Agents could stop valid historical or registered-source acquisition routes because top-level supports_live/ready_for_live fields conflict with source_acquisition_policy live/historical flags.
repair: AGENTS.md now directs agents to reconcile live versus historical acquisition through repo_source_data_acquisition_policy, source_registry, and production_data_pull_rule_sheet, and to record contradictions instead of stopping unrelated valid routes.
SKILL.md now states source readiness may be ambiguous until the source-acquisition repair wave lands.
pyproject.toml mode_semantics records source readiness reconciliation before blocking configured acquisition routes.
findings_and_repairs.optimized_fix_plan_effect: Wave 2 remains the semantic repair owner, while current research data acquisition can continue through valid configured routes with contradiction evidence.

findings_and_repairs.title: Stale repo-index and generated freshness states could be interpreted as hard workflow failures.
findings_and_repairs.risk_before_patch: The required preflight intentionally runs repo-index status, but the audit found generated index artifacts can make status stale even when the index head matches the current commit.
repair: AGENTS.md now says stale generated artifact drift is evidence, not failure by itself.
SKILL.md now classifies stale index status, generated freshness drift, exact-path misses, scanner findings, and handoff text as evidence unless a required focused test or validator fails.
pyproject.toml repo_index now has status_semantics, stale_status_is_blocking false, blocking_status_conditions, and known_nonblocking_stale_reasons.
findings_and_repairs.optimized_fix_plan_effect: Wave 10 read-only/index cleanup can fix the root behavior, but preflight/status noise no longer halts unrelated valid work.

findings_and_repairs.title: Audit-only commit rule was too absolute for a repo whose repair sequencing is driven by distilled crack graphs.
findings_and_repairs.risk_before_patch: A useful architecture/crack-graph commit could be rejected as audit-only even when it materially changes future workflow and prevents repeated repo resets.
repair: AGENTS.md now says to avoid index-only/audit-only/status-only commits unless the commit is a distilled architectural decision artifact that materially changes repair sequencing or future workflow.
SKILL.md and pyproject.toml now encode the same distinction between distilled architecture/crack graphs and raw audit-log churn.
findings_and_repairs.optimized_fix_plan_effect: The strategy plan and crack graph can become durable route evidence without opening the door to noisy raw report dumps.

findings_and_repairs.title: Helper catalog usage was too trusting despite known helper-manifest drift.
findings_and_repairs.risk_before_patch: Agents could stop or duplicate work because the helper catalog lacks current scripts and script_surface_usage_policy leaves current scripts unclassified.
repair: AGENTS.md now says to prefer helper/builder/validator scripts when the helper catalog is current for that surface.

AGENTS.md and SKILL.md now say to verify current files and the script import architecture index when helper metadata is missing or stale, then repair the catalog or make the smallest owner-surface edit.
findings_and_repairs.optimized_fix_plan_effect: Wave 8 remains responsible for full helper reconciliation, but missing helper catalog entries no longer stop Wave 1 through Wave 7 work.

findings_and_repairs.title: Real-data smoke language could be read as a blocker for research design and overlay implementation.
findings_and_repairs.risk_before_patch: The docs said real-data smoke is required before treating a VBT tool as useful beyond catalog/import authority, which could block docs lookup, schema design, dry-run planning, or overlay work.
repair: SKILL.md now says real-data smoke is required before treating a VBT tool as proven for data-backed research claims beyond catalog/import authority.
SKILL.md now says missing smoke is an evidence gap, not a blocker for docs lookup, schema design, dry-run planning, or overlay implementation.
findings_and_repairs.optimized_fix_plan_effect: VBT Data bridge, portfolio modes, VBT642 overlay, and VBT tool boundary work can be designed and tested before real-data smoke promotes any data-backed claim.

findings_and_repairs.title: pyproject.toml did not declare jsonschema despite active validators/tests using Draft202012Validator.
findings_and_repairs.risk_before_patch: Wave 1 activation schema enforcement and existing validators rely on jsonschema, but the project dependency list only declared fastjsonschema in optional indexing dependencies.
repair: Added jsonschema>=4.22 to project dependencies.
findings_and_repairs.optimized_fix_plan_effect: Wave 1 can use Draft202012Validator as a first-class project dependency instead of relying on incidental environment packages.

findings_and_repairs.title: Root docs still used production wording in ways that could confuse research artifact boundaries.
findings_and_repairs.risk_before_patch: Users and agents could confuse production namespace names with production-ready claims.
repair: AGENTS.md and SKILL.md now define production-in-paths as historical namespace or operational-control wording, not readiness.
SKILL.md title changed from Production Skill to Operational Research Skill.
pyproject.toml project description changed from controlled salvage rebuild root to operational research rebuild root.
Qdrant/native lookup language now says required evidence for the specific research/runtime/data-acquisition/promotion claim, not generic production evidence.
findings_and_repairs.optimized_fix_plan_effect: Portfolio/optimizer and VBT Data outputs are now clearly research artifacts until a separate promotion route proves otherwise.

remaining_control_file_risks.description: AGENTS.md and SKILL.md still contain many historical paths and command names with production in them. This is acceptable after the namespace clarification, but future renaming would be a larger compatibility project.
pyproject.toml now encodes stale-index semantics, but repo-index scripts do not yet enforce those semantics. That remains implementation work.

Root docs now point at the strategy plan under tmp/reports/production. If the plan is not committed, future agents may not see it. Either commit the distilled plan or move it to an approved durable plan root during Wave 0.
Root docs classify catalog_reference versus activated_private_runtime, but the actual VBT642/tool schemas and validators are not mode-aware yet.

optimized_fix_plan_adjustments.change: Promote Wave 0 from optional hygiene to required before committing root control-file changes.
optimized_fix_plan_adjustments.reason: The control files now reference the distilled strategy plan; that plan should either be committed with these edits or moved to a durable plan root.

optimized_fix_plan_adjustments.change: Keep Waves 1, 2, and 3 as the locked sequential foundation.
optimized_fix_plan_adjustments.reason: Root instructions now explicitly route activation, source acquisition, and overlay contradictions through those waves.

optimized_fix_plan_adjustments.change: Allow Wave 8 and Wave 10 to run in parallel when they stop creating false blockers for Waves 1 through 7.
optimized_fix_plan_adjustments.reason: Helper drift and stale-index noise are now classified as evidence, but the underlying scripts still need cleanup.

optimized_fix_plan_adjustments.change: Treat real-data smoke as claim evidence, not design permission.
optimized_fix_plan_adjustments.reason: VBT Data bridge, VBT642 overlay, and VBT tool boundary design should not wait for live or historical pulls; smoke is required before claiming the tool is proven on data.

validation_performed.command: Preflight authority query and guidance map lookup
pyproject tomllib parse after first-pass patch

next_validation_required: python -m json.tool on changed JSON reports/plans
secret-token-pattern scan on changed root/report files

bottom_line.start_wave: Wave 1 activation_schema_validator_tests after committing or otherwise preserving Wave 0 strategy/control-file evidence
